checklist.question: Did receivables grow materially faster than revenue across consecutive periods?

evidence.location: 10-K FY2010, Balance Sheet - Receivables, net current

checklist.question: Did non-current 'soft' assets (intangibles, other assets, capitalized items) grow materially faster than the business overall?

evidence.quote: OtherAssetsNoncurrent=615000000 (2009-04-03) growing to OtherAssetsNoncurrent=773000000 (2010-04-02), a +25.7% increase, while Revenues fell from 16740000000 (FY2009) to 16128000000 (FY2010), a -3.7% decline
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010, Balance Sheet - Other assets, noncurrent

evidence.quote: Goodwill=3784000000 (2009-04-03) to Goodwill=3866000000 (2010-04-02), +2.2% growth against a revenue decline of -3.7%

checklist.question: Is there a persistent divergence between net income and operating cash flow?

evidence.quote: NetIncomeLoss=817000000 (FY2010) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1643000000 (FY2010); NetIncomeLoss=1115000000 (FY2009) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1986000000 (FY2009); NetIncomeLoss=545000000 (FY2008) vs NetCashProvidedByUsedInOperatingActivities=1343000000 (FY2008) — operating cash flow consistently exceeds net income, the opposite of an earnings-quality red flag
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010, Income Statement and Cash Flow Statement

checklist.question: Do inventory levels or gross margins show trends inconsistent with revenue?

evidence.quote: No InventoryNet concept provided (CSC is a services company); gross margin computed from Revenues=16500000000 and CostOfServices=13150000000 (FY2008, ~20.3% margin), Revenues=16740000000 and CostOfServices=13268000000 (FY2009, ~20.7% margin), Revenues=16128000000 and CostOfServices=12797000000 (FY2010, ~20.7% margin) — margins are stable and track revenue changes reasonably
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010, Income Statement - Cost of services

checklist.question: Is the earnings trajectory unusually smooth relative to the volatility of revenue and cash flow?

evidence.quote: Quarterly NetIncomeLoss values show a large spike: NetIncomeLoss=121000000 (Q1 FY2009, 2008-03-29 to 2008-07-04), NetIncomeLoss=452000000 (Q2 FY2009, 2008-07-05 to 2008-10-03), NetIncomeLoss=161000000 (Q3 FY2009, 2008-10-04 to 2009-01-02) — earnings volatility exceeds the relatively stable quarterly Revenues (4437000000, 4239000000, 3952000000 for the same quarters), the opposite of unusual smoothness
evidence.location: 10-Q Q2 FY2009 and 10-Q Q3 FY2009 Income Statements

checklist.question: Did liability, allowance, or reserve balances decline (or fail to grow) despite growing business activity?

evidence.quote: AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3786000000 (2009-04-03) and AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=3849000000 (2010-04-02) are identical in magnitude to ReceivablesNetCurrent for the same dates, while AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent=55000000 (2009-07-03) is far smaller and more plausible as an actual allowance figure — this internal inconsistency suggests a tagging/data error rather than a reliable reserve trend
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010 XBRL tag AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent vs 10-Q FY2010 Q1

checklist.question: Does the filing chronology show irregularities (late filings/NT forms, amendments, unusual 8-K frequency)?

evidence.location: Filing chronology - notification of late filing forms 2006-2007
Filing chronology - multiple amended periodic reports 2002-2008

checklist.question: Is the provided data sufficient in coverage and length for the above assessments?

evidence.quote: Quarterly XBRL detail is available only from 2008-03-29 (Q1 FY2009) through 2010-04-02 (FY2010 year-end), i.e., roughly two fiscal years, with only three full annual Revenues data points (FY2008=16500000000, FY2009=16740000000, FY2010=16128000000); no InventoryNet, no segment-level receivable aging, and an apparently erroneous AllowanceForDoubtfulAccountsReceivableCurrent series limit the depth of trend analysis
evidence.location: 10-K FY2010 and prior 10-Q filings - data coverage

mechanism_hypotheses.accounting_treatment: Possible under-impairment or continued capitalization of costs (e.g., deferred contract costs, capitalized software, or transition/setup costs on outsourcing contracts) into Other Assets, Noncurrent, causing this line to grow +25.7% (615000000 to 773000000) even as consolidated Revenues declined -3.7% (16740000000 to 16128000000) in the same fiscal year, which could mask deteriorating recoverability of long-lived/intangible balances.

Potential revenue recognition timing acceleration (e.g., percentage-of-completion estimates on long-term outsourcing/systems-integration contracts) causing receivables to grow modestly (3786000000 to 3849000000) even while top-line revenue contracted, implying slower cash conversion of recognized revenue relative to sales activity.

If the allowance figures tagged at 3786000000 (2009-04-03) and 3849000000 (2010-04-02) reflect gross receivables rather than an actual bad-debt reserve (as suggested by the much smaller 55000000 figure at 2009-07-03), the true allowance for doubtful accounts may be understated relative to receivables outstanding, understating bad-debt expense and overstating net receivables/assets — though this could also be a pure XBRL tagging error rather than a substantive misstatement.

rationale_evidence.location: 10-K FY2010 and 10-Q Q1 FY2010 XBRL allowance tag

top_signals: Other Assets, Noncurrent grew +25.7% (615000000 to 773000000, FY2009→FY2010) while Revenues fell -3.7% (16740000000 to 16128000000), a soft-asset/revenue divergence
Receivables grew +1.7% (3786000000 to 3849000000) despite declining Revenues (-3.7%), a mild working-capital divergence

History of multiple NT 10-Q/NT 10-K late-filing notices (2006-2007) and periodic-report amendments (10-K/A, 10-Q/A) plus recurring SEC CORRESP/UPLOAD correspondence through 2009, indicating a pattern of financial-reporting process friction and SEC scrutiny

Anomalous/likely mis-tagged Allowance for Doubtful Accounts data (values matching gross receivables in two periods vs. a much smaller figure in a third), reducing confidence in reserve adequacy assessment
Operating cash flow consistently exceeds net income across FY2008-FY2010, which is a mitigating (not aggravating) factor for earnings-quality concerns
